108.status: Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Salt Lake on July 17; went 1-for-2 with a walk. Scheduled to play again on July 19.
Started a rehab assignment with Triple-A Salt Lake on July 19.
Left his outing in the Arizona Complex League on July 18, but the injury isn't considered serious.
Began a rehab assignment on July 7 in the Arizona Complex League and threw a scoreless inning with three strikeouts on July 11. Getting closer to getting up to pitch for an affiliate.

Started his throwing program and has progressed to playing catch with a targeted return date shortly after the All-Star break. Threw a bullpen session on July 17 and is scheduled to face hitters on July 21.
Underwent CT scan on June 10 that revealed the fracture and underwent surgery with a timeline of roughly four weeks. Progressed to catching and hitting at the club's complex in Arizona.

Participated in light on-field activity in early June but ultimately decided to undergo right knee surgery. Can't return until July 21 at the earliest, and his surgery is expected to keep him out roughly four to six weeks.

Said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. MRI exam on June 8 revealed a torn ulnar collateral ligament. Underwent successful season-ending Tommy John surgery.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Felt discomfort warming up in bullpen on July 1. Underwent MRI, which revealed no ligament damage and inflammation around UCL. Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day IL on July 19.

Received facet injection to lower back on June 12, then underwent minor procedure to have bone spur removed shortly after. He has been playing catch on the mound and expects to throw a bullpen session early the week of July 19.
Underwent MRI on June 30, which revealed injury to left UCL. Scheduled to meet with Dr. Keith Meister in Arlington, Texas, on July 14.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continued to discuss treatment options. Scheduled to be evaluated by Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles in late July.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Optimistic he'll be ready for start of Spring Training 2027. Nearing a running progression as of June 23.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3. Progressing to his first bullpen session on June 23.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Running in Sarasota as of June 13.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.

111.status: The possibility of Suarez returning when first eligible on July 21 remains in play as the lefty threw a side session prior to the game on July 19.
Will continue to ramp up catch-play program in coming days with an eye toward a bullpen session after that, and possibly a live BP and/or rehab start.
Seems to be all set from hitting perspective. Key will be responding well to intense defensive drills that require mobility and lateral movement. Could be reasonably close to rehab assignment.
Scheduled to get follow-up X-rays and visit with hand specialist on July 20. Timetable will be more clear then.
Scheduled to get follow-up X-rays and visit with hand specialist on July 20. Timetable will be more clear then.
Continues to do strengthening exercises for rotator cuff. No plans yet to start throwing.
Will throw first bullpen session since the injury the week of July 20.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Has started throwing two bullpen sessions per week in Fort Myers, Fla.
Has resumed throwing, starting at a distance of 100 feet.
Shut down from swinging a bat due to a recent wrist hiccup. He will be checked before restarting progression.

112.status: Set to be activated to start against Detroit on July 20, per manager Craig Counsell. Logged 4 2/3 scoreless innings (53 pitches) in rehab outing for Triple-A Iowa on July 11.

Injured June 23 stretching to receive a throw at first base. Has resumed throwing, plus running and agility work. Threw bullpen session on July 16 and live BP could follow late the following week, per manager Craig Counsell.
Resumed a throwing program (plyo ball work) on June 29. Moved rehab to Arizona, but has not made much progress, per manager Craig Counsell. Has had to repeat steps in throwing program.
Has started to increase activity to tolerance, including plyo ball work. Has been "asymptomatic" for a few days, per Craig Counsell, but Cubs continuing to be conservative.

Resumed throwing June 22, but unlikely to return as a starter this season. Met with Dr. Keith Meister on July 7, gaining clearance to keep building. Goal is to get off a mound the first week of August.
Threw off a mound in a bullpen session on July 8, per manager Craig Counsell. Will likely have another bullpen workout before the All-Star break.
Has continued to build up in a throwing program in Arizona. Manager Craig Counsell said the lefty's timetable potentially points to an August return.
Has resumed a throwing program in Arizona. Harvey's timetable for return remains unclear, given the previous starts and stops within his comeback bid to date.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Has resumed a throwing program in Arizona. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 15 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.
Appendix flared up overnight and Milner required an appendectomy on June 27, per manager Craig Counsell. Estimated timetable is 4-6 weeks.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.

114.status: Pitched scoreless inning in July 18 rehab appearance with Triple-A Columbus. Took a line drive off his elbow on July 8. Not expected to miss significant time.
Began rehab assignment on July 17 with Triple-A Columbus. Will progressively build up during each rehab outing.
Has begun facing live pitching. Might not require a rehab assignment before his activation. Underwent surgery on June 16. Remains on initial return timeline of five to seven weeks.

114.injury: Tommy John surgery and right flexor tendon repair
114.status: Threw live batting practice on July 15 and set to do so again on July 20. Recovering from June 2025 surgery. Signed two-year Minor League deal on Feb. 6.
Underwent right UCL repair surgery on July 6 with Dr. Keith Meister. Typical return-to-play timeline is 10 to 12 months.

115.status: Says the pain is at the front of the shoulder, and he hopes to receive an MRI before devising a rehab plan.
Feeling good enough that he hopes to face hitters at Coors Field sometime the week of July 20.
Reached 101.6 mph while facing hitters at Coors Field on July 16; scheduled to begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Albuquerque on July 19.
Did not feel good after a bullpen session, so he rested over the All-Star break and is restarting his comeback with light catch sessions.

115.injury: Left oblique contusion, left groin tightness
115.status: Tests in the weight room on July 17 went well enough that he is looking forward to pool workouts and running. Swinging the bat and throwing, but getting above "90 percent" intensity is a hurdle.
Played catch during the All-Star break and is cleared to begin throwing at 125 feet "with intensity."
Underwent an internal brace and repair operation on July 1.
Had surgery June 22 to have an internal brace put in his elbow. Expected recovery is one year.More >
Underwent an MRI June 16 as doctors try to get a handle on what has stopped his rehab from advancing.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 17 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Exited his start on July 19 after taking a comebacker off left hand/wrist area. Team is unconcerned with the wrist, but will reevaluate groin on July 20.
Scheduled to pitch for Double-A Erie on July 19.
Playing catch and completing rehab daily.
Underwent left UCL reconstruction surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on July 15.
Completing a return-to-play progression for hitting, running and fielding. Struck by therapy band below left eye during postgame arm-care routine June 16.
Threw 16 pitches in an inning-long rehab appearance with Single-A Lakeland on July 17, allowing two runs on a walk and a hit.
Continued a rehab assignment with Single-A Lakeland on July 17, going 0-for-3 at the plate with a walk and playing seven innings at second base.
Diagnosed with stress fracture of the olecranon, a bone at the tip of the elbow, after reporting soreness June 27. Will not require surgery but will need to rest. Expected to pitch again this season.
Threw a bullpen session on July 9. Will play catch while at the All-Star Game in Philadelphia.
Began a throwing progression on June 15.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9. Fracture is healing slowly, according to A.J. Hinch.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Will be reinstated from the IL on July 20 and make his first start of 2026 against the Marlins.
Burrows is working out at the team's facility in West Palm Beach and will get stretched out as a starter.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Could return to the Astros' rotation July 24 at the White Sox or make one more rehab start at Triple-A Sugar Land.
Could play July 20 after missing one game with his injury suffered July 18.
Grabbed his right side following a check-swing in the fourth inning, then was replaced by LaMonte Wade Jr.
Banged his left knee into the wall trying to make a catch in the first inning of the July 12 game in Arlington against the Rangers and was removed for a pinch-hitter.
Threw eight pitches in one inning on a Minor League rehab outing July 11 at Triple-A Sugar Land and had another pair of scoreless, one-inning outings on July 13 and 15 for FCL Astros.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: General back tightness progressed through July 18 game against the Padres, so he was held out of lineup on July 19 as a precaution. Will see how he's feeling on July 20 to determine status for game.

Experienced forearm tightness while making a rehab start July 18 as he makes his way back from the restricted list and an extended absence. Imaging scheduled for July 20 to determine severity and next steps.
Most recent setback occurred on July 6, and a July 10 scan showed a right lat strain. Won't throw until July 26. Transferred to 60-day IL on July 14.
After minor setback that paused rehab before the All-Star break, participated in full team workout July 17. If progression continues, could begin rehab assignment week of July 21.
Scratched from rehab start July 1 with shoulder soreness. Received cortisone injection July 2 and hasn't thrown since, but could begin new throwing program week of July 20.
Suffered strain June 30 and is continuing his rehab program in Arizona.
Suffered setback June 27 and received injection July 1. Has started throwing program again as of July 17.

Began swinging again during All-Star break and will be evaluated again July 19 to see if he's ready to ramp up his hitting progression, which would include full rounds of batting practice rather than a modified version.
Underwent a UCL repair on left elbow July 1 and facing a 10-12 month return to play timeline.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Will not make his next scheduled pitching start on July 22, and could miss future starts to allow his knee to recover. The Dodgers expect him to pitch again this season.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City on July 18, tossing 1 1/3 innings on 32 pitches.
Made his third rehab outing with Single-A Ontario on July 17.
Shut down from baseball activities because his neck was not recovering well, with the hope of starting back up during the week of July 20.
Continuing his bullpen progression and still only throwing fastballs.
Has resumed playing catch, with a slow progression in the works.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back surgery
119.status: Underwent a surgical procedure on his back, he shared on social media.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Hadn't pitched since July 11, but injury flared back up on July 18, leading to IL placement. Will undergo imaging.
Progressing with strengthening activity as tolerated. Placed on 15-day IL on June 29 before being transferred to 60-day IL on July 19.
Underwent surgery on July 17 after trying to play through pain. "It's not too long of a recovery process," manager Blake Butera said.
Made first rehab appearance on July 13 for Rookie-level FCL Nationals, pitching three scoreless innings. Threw a bullpen session on July 16.
Strengthening and playing catch from 75 feet.
Struck out five in two innings of one-run ball for Rookie-level FCL Nats on July 14. Threw a bullpen session on July 16.
Progressing with throwing activity as tolerated from the Nationals' training complex.
Progressing with throwing activity as tolerated. Had been shut down after an MRI revealed a left flexor strain following his June 16 rehab start.
Parker most likely will undergo Tommy John surgery. He exited in the bottom of the seventh inning on June 28, after issuing a four-pitch walk in which he appeared in noticeable discomfort.
Threw off the mound for the first time last week, on schedule with throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
On schedule with continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL.

121.status: Scheduled to begin a Minor League rehab assignment July 18, but rain prevented that from happening. Threw a roughly 45-pitch bullpen session instead; tentatively slated to pitch in the Minors around July 23. Candidate for activation beginning five days after that.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 30 and will continue it at least through the weekend of July 18-19. Could be activated for the team's July 20-22 series in Milwaukee.

Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 25 and advanced to Triple-A Syracuse on July 8. Could apply for an extension of his assignment beyond the 30-day limit, which is often granted for pitchers recovering from Tommy John surgery.
Struck on the right hand by a 92.2 mph Michael Wacha sinker on July 9. Will not require surgery but is expected to miss six to eight weeks.
Felt discomfort during a July 7 appearance against the Royals and went for an MRI the next day, which revealed no ligament damage. Will rest for around five days before restarting a throwing program.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Is only participating in range of motion and strengthening exercises at this time.
Started range of motion exercises. Stitches will be removed on July 21, at which point he will begin baseball activities as able.
Continuing running, throwing and swinging progressions.
Progressing with plyometric exercises and looks to begin a throwing progression soon.
MRI on July 18 showed wear and tear of the articulate cartilage on the medial femoral condyle. No baseball activities at this time.
Underwent successful left knee arthroscopic surgery on July 17. Expected to be ready for 2027 Spring Training.
Began a throwing progression on June 11. No timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Removed from game June 24 with hamstring discomfort after running the bases on a double-play ball in the third inning. Diagnosed with strain after ultrasound and MRI. Expected to begin rehab assignment week of July 27.
Placed on IL after injury continued to act up after initially popping up Fourth of July weekend. Began hitting in Pirate City on July 17 and is expected to begin rehab assignment week of July 27.

Sisk began experiencing some discomfort during June 29-July 5 road trip. Pirates expect to learn more about the extent of the injury when they return home. No surgery needed and expected to begin throwing progression on July 18.

134.injury: Left hand fourth and fifth metacarpal non-displaced fractures
134.status: Cleared to begin gripping activities June 22. No longer wearing cast on left hand. Expected to start rehab assignment at start of August.

134.injury: Torn left ring finger sagittal band
134.status: Injured the finger on a diving play in Pittsburgh's game on July 5. After imaging, the injury is to the left hand ring finger sagittal band, and will sideline Griffin until at least September. He'll have a splint for six weeks.

Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster. Began side progression the first week of June. Expected to throw live batting practice first week of July.

135.status: Sidelined after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Threw an up-down bullpen July 17 in Arizona and felt good after the session.

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Threw an up-down bullpen July 17 and felt good after the session.
Sent for further testing to determine the grade of the strain, though Taylor is hopeful it isn't serious. Injury occurred on a swing during the Padres' July 8 game vs. Arizona.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May and landed on the IL when it worsened. Has resumed throwing bullpens -- albeit with no plan for a rehab stint yet.
Placed on the IL after pitching through left knee soreness in multiple outings. Began throwing progression week of July 6.
Struck by a comebacker. Discharged from hospital after fainting while on his way to an X-ray. Passed all tests related to fainting incident. X-rays on ankle were also negative.
MRI revealed no major structural damage, merely a strain in the subscapularis muscle. Adam expressed hope to return in early-to-mid August.
Experienced elbow soreness after his outing on June 21. Resumed light throwing in late June.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Made first start in the field on rehab assignment -- at second base on July 19 at Triple-A Tacoma -- after DH'ing over his first two games, on July 17 and July 15. Expected to move around defensively in anticipation of utility role upon activation.

Recently began throwing program, which is expected to include ramp up in intensity with each session. Current injury is a more serious strain in a different part of his lat than his injury earlier this season.More »
Was about a week behind Brash with throwing progression as of July 17 update.
Slated to throw bullpen session on July 21, soon after which he could begin a Minor League rehab assignment, if all goes well.
Exited in the second inning vs. Rays on July 12 after reaching out to grab 107.2 mph comebacker in the first inning. X-rays were negative.
Pulled off rehab assignment due to pain in his finger. Meeting with doctor, and if injury isn't making progress, he'll need season-ending surgery.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Remains in San Francisco and is continuing to rehab. Could return during the Giants' next homestand from July 24-29.
Currently rehabbing in San Francisco. Remains shut down from baseball activity.
Continuing all baseball activities, including his baserunning progression.
Received PRP injection on July 11 and will be reevaluated in four weeks.
Has resumed throwing four times per week out to 105 feet.
Suffered a setback with hamstring tightness and will be limited to throwing off flat ground through the weekend of July 10-12.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Woke up with appendicitis on July 16 and had emergency surgery. Manager Oliver Marmol said everything went well but wanted to wait to speak with Romero before discussing a timeline for return.
Will go for a second opinion on a right elbow issue. Transfer to 60-day IL implies a lengthy absence is anticipated.
Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL.

139.status: Began a rehab stint with Triple-A Durham on July 9. Last pitched two innings out of the bullpen for Triple-A Durham on July 17, striking out two while throwing 36 pitches. Expected to make a few one-inning appearances before rejoining Rays.

Felt soreness while pitching on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation. Threw 29 pitches over three innings in a rehab start for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays on July 7. Joined Triple-A Durham to start July 18.

Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Pitched a scoreless inning for the Rookie-level FCL Rays on July 10, then walked two in a scoreless inning for Triple-A Durham on July 17.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury. Had rehab assignment stalled by sprained ankle April 11, then shut down May 12 due to left shoulder inflammation. Started rehab assignment with Triple-A Durham on June 30 but shut down again after receiving an injection in his right shoulder.
Had rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Durham on June 30 and played three more games, but was pulled off due to continued discomfort in his groin area. Returned to the Tampa Bay area.
Had a PRP injection after a setback while playing catch. Still not throwing. Will visit a doctor soon. "The rehab has not gone as well as we would have hoped," manager Kevin Cash said.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, 2025. Faced hitters in live batting practice for the first time on June 30. Will soon join Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays to begin a rehab assignment.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he suffered while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 8.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

140.status: Is going to start hitting soft toss in the cage on July 19 after beginning regular hitting progressions off the tee the previous day. He’ll also begin to take ground balls.
Threw a bullpen session over the All-Star break and continues throwing off flat ground in Atlanta. Arm feels good as he continues rehabbing after ankle surgery.
Is making his third trip to the injured list with the same injury this season.
Expected to make first rehab outing with Double-A Frisco on July 12 ahead of the All-Star break.

Montgomery looked sharp on July 7, scattering five hits and recording three strikeouts without issuing a walk for Triple-A Round Rock, throwing 40 pitches (26 strikes) and averaging 90.7 mph with his sinker.
Elbow flared up during rehab assignment and elected to have surgery. Will miss the remainder of the 2026 season.
Underwent surgery to repair a herniated disk in his neck on June 29. Expected to begin baseball activities in about three weeks.
Suffered multiple fractures on the pinky finger of his right hand after being hit by a pitch in June 13 game in Boston. Will receive additional X-rays/scans when the team returns home.
Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Received three shots in his right thumb over the break and will continue to ramp up throwing after that brief "pause."
Has still not begun any baseball activity.
Has started throwing bullpen sessions again in an effort to return this season, should enter rehab games soon.
Facing live pitching and should soon progress to rehab games.
Received another cortisone shot after feeling discomfort in his shoulder in mid-July.
Made first rehab appearance on June 16, but was scratched from second planned rehab appearance with a neck issue, per John Schneider.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Pitched three shutout innings with three strikeouts in rehab start for Triple-A St. Paul on July 19.

Continues to increase baseball activities daily, including defensive work and hitting. Club hopes he will be ready to return at some point during the upcoming series in Cleveland, pending how he responds to workouts.

Threw two innings in a rehab outing on July 17 and is scheduled for a one-inning appearance on July 21. Club plans to continue building his workload, potentially pitching two of every three days before activation.

Underwent surgery on July 1. Will be re-evaluated around Sept. 1 (approximately eight weeks post-op) to determine when he can resume throwing, which is expected to occur sometime between eight and 12 weeks after surgery.
Threw on July 18 and is scheduled for a two-inning, 35-pitch rehab outing on July 21.
Scheduled for a two-inning, 35-pitch rehab outing on July 22 as he continues his progression in Fort Myers.

Experienced a recurrence of shoulder symptoms after bullpen sessions following his stem cell injection. Will visit Dr. Bauers in Atlanta this week for further evaluation after being unable to progress to live batting practice.
Continuing rehab activities in Fort Myers. The organization is not expressing concern and expects him to return as soon as he’s ready.

Suffered injury while running out a groundout on July 17. Was held out of lineup on July 18 as a precaution, but was in "pretty good shape" after completing baseball activities. Will continue to be evaluated.
Underwent surgery to repair injured lat and will not pitch again in 2026
Is throwing from 105 feet and has begun hitting off a tee and in soft toss.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.

143.status: Will get a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister before deciding on potential surgery. Interim manager Don Mattingly said he expects Keller to miss the rest of the season.
Landed on the IL after allowing five runs over 1 1/3 innings in his first two appearances after being recalled from Triple-A Lehigh Valley on July 4. He has a 7.14 ERA this season.
The Phillies placed Trivino on the IL before their July 8 game in Cincinnati. There is no immediate timetable for his return.

Reinstated from the restricted list on June 25 and placed on the 60-day IL. Experienced right elbow discomfort while ramping up baseball activities for return from 80-game suspension. Underwent season-ending surgery June 19.
Injured on June 10 after attempting to throw out a runner at the plate. Underwent season-ending surgery on June 24. A free agent at season's end, García is expected to be fully ready for 2027 season.

143.injury: Discogenic pain and facet inflammation in back
143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.

144.status: Played right field for the first time during rehab stint on July 19, going 0-for-2 with sacrifice fly for Triple-A Gwinnett. Return to Atlanta is not yet imminent.
Went 1-for-4 with a walk as the designated hitter in rehab game for Triple-A Gwinnett on July 19. Went on IL after experiencing inflammation in finger that was surgically repaired in January.
Struck out five over 4 1/3 scoreless innings in Triple-A Gwinnett’s win over Toledo in Game 1 of a seven-inning doubleheader on July 18.
Manager Walt Weiss said Yastrzemski's stay on the injured list will likely be a short one. The outfielder is eligible for activation on July 21.
Resumed throwing after the All-Star break, but the Braves still haven't decided when he will begin a rehab assignment.
Manager Walt Weiss said Perez could be activated before the current homestand ends on July 23. The lefty seems to be a strong candidate to start the July 22 game against San Diego.
Will likely be sidelined through the end of July, according to manager Walt Weiss, and may not return until August.
Evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister on June 15 and prescribed one month of rest. If his MRI is clean when he is re-evaluated in July, will be cleared to begin throwing progressions.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. Was in New York on June 13 for a doctor's visit when Atlanta played the Mets.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.

145.status: Threw two scoreless innings and struck out three for the ACL White Sox on July 18 in his first rehab start as he continues to work back from Tommy John surgery and a recent appendectomy.
Set to begin a rehab assignment on July 20 after suffering a concussion while making a leaping catch in mid-June.
After being pulled from a rehab assignment on June 1, Hays began a new rehab stint on July 17 with Triple-A Charlotte.
Has been participating in long toss after having a setback in his rehab process. His timeline is uncertain at this point.

Will start throwing again in the next 1-2 weeks after his first injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
Threw two scoreless innings with two strikeouts for High-A Winston-Salem on July 1.
Threw one scoreless inning with two strikeouts for High-A Winston-Salem on July 1.
Replaced on the roster by fellow southpaw Chris Murphy.
Felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Day to day. Finger hit by grounder on July 18 but remained in game. Played catch July 19. Hoping for inflammation and soreness to decrease.
Resumed throwing and light baseball activity. Had been managing the issue, which is similar to the one he had in 2025, all season. Got to the point that he needed to rest it.
Resumed pitch designs and is working through running progression. Was going through pitching drills in the outfield and stepped awkwardly to roll his ankle on July 8.
Resumed baseball activity and light conditioning work. Felt some tightness on July 7, then good pregame July 8. Tightness came back on defense enough that he exited the July 8 game.

Cleared to resume throwing and working through throwing progression. First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.
Has live pitch designs as part of late stages of return-to-play progression. Injured during warmup drills on May 31. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 21.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Throwing at 60 feet. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.
No-throw rehab. Had been feeling something off and on in his arm for several weeks but got to the point where it affected him enough to say something.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 3 prsopect; No. 41 overall)
146.status: Hadn't played since June 26. Previously missed time due to core muscle surgery and right wrist soreness.

146.playerName: Thomas White (Marlins' No. 1 prospect; No. 21 overall)
146.status: Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.More >

146.playerName: Robby Snelling (Marlins' No. 2 prospect; No. 38 overall)
146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

147.status: Threw 52 pitches in Minor League rehab start for Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre on July 17. Will need at least one more rehab start. Has not pitched in Majors since May 13 vs. Baltimore.

Tossed approximately 10 pitches from a mound on July 16, then underwent a second round of PRP injections, manager Aaron Boone said. The Yankees said Rodón has continued throwing and aims to complete a bullpen session in the next several days.
Pitched one inning of live batting practice July 18, throwing 15 fastballs to Anthony Volpe and Ali Sánchez at Yankee Stadium. Next outing will incorporate breaking pitches.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: Underwent re-imaging July 15, which showed healing, but not enough to resume baseball activities. Additional re-imaging required. Has been performing mostly lower-body exercises since last playing May 31. Initially placed on 10-day IL June 5, retroactive to June 2.
Has resumed outdoor running after sustaining a new strain to the right calf while running bases in June at Yankee Stadium. Has undergone several PRP injections to speed healing.

147.playerName: Carlos Lagrange (Yankees' No. 4 prospect, MLB's No. 83)
147.status: Based on initial timeline, would resume throwing program in mid-August. “It’s going to be a close call” for him to help at the big league level this year, Brian Cashman said.
Resumed throwing weekend of June 13-14. Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

158.status: Threw 15 pitches in a scoreless inning for High-A Wisconsin on July 19 to begin a rehab assignment and told reporters there, "it couldn't have gone much better."

Underwent a precautionary MRI scan over the All-Star break and reported that the results were "all good." Threw a bullpen session on July 18 and is on track to return soon after his IL stint expires July 24.
Will undergo a contrast MRI to determine the severity of an injury that has bothered him for the past two months, but became unbearable during a July 17 win over the Marlins.
Announced July 18 that he will undergo surgery that will include a repair of the capsule in his right shoulder, similar to the procedure that sidelined him for about 20 months from 2023-25.
Scheduled to travel to Triple-A Nashville on July 20 to begin a rehab assignment with back-to-back games on July 21-22, according to manager Pat Murphy.
Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville on July 21, per manager Pat Murphy.
Sent for a rehab assignment July 3 to the Arizona Complex League. Moved rehab July 10 to Triple-A Nashville.
Acquired from the A's in June, made 10 Brewers appearances in various roles before going down with injury.
Underwent Tommy John surgery on July 1 after receiving a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister.
Underwent successful but season-ending thoracic outlet decompression surgery on June 22 in Dallas. Based on a typical recovery, should be ready for the start of next season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11.
